directive.title: Mobile Responsive — Per-Page Chart & Data-Viz Reflow

auditor.summary: The build implements the core requirements for responsive chart components with a shared ResponsiveChartWrapper, useChartDimensions hook, centralized config, and mobile-optimized styles. However, there are critical issues that prevent this from passing: (1) React.memo syntax is invalid and will cause TypeScript compilation errors, (2) tickInterval calculation has a bug that can violate the spacing constraint, (3) heatmap tooltip positioning may still clip on mobile Safari. The build is recoverable with fixes to these issues. Score is 72 due to the critical compilation error and high-severity logic bugs.

top_issues.description: React.memo is used with incorrect syntax. React.memo<Props>({ ... }) is invalid - React.memo expects a function component, not an object literal. This will cause TypeScript compilation errors and runtime failures.

tickInterval calculation is incorrect. The formula Math.ceil(dataLength / Math.floor(containerWidth / 60)) - 1 can produce intervals that violate the constraint (dataLength / tickInterval) * 60 <= containerWidth. When dataLength=20 and containerWidth=393, tickInterval=3, but (20/3)*60=400 > 393.
Heatmap tooltip uses window.innerWidth for positioning which doesn't account for scroll position or iOS safe areas. The tooltip could still be clipped on mobile Safari.
